channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Italy PM Meloni visits pope in hospital, says he's in good spirits as tests show slight improvement
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis for 20 minutes in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rahm Emanuel: Trump helping achieve Stalin’s dream with Russia-Ukraine comments
item.description: Former U.S. Ambassador to Japan Rahm Emanuel said President Trump is helping to achieve former Soviet Union dictator Joseph Stalin’s dream with his recent actions surrounding the Russia-Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Advance of Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo threatens regional peace, UN officials warn
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Top United Nations officials in Africa warned an emergency meeting of the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday that the offensive of Rwanda-backed rebels in mineral-rich eastern Congo is threatening peace in the wider region.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Labor unions call on Trump to boost US shipbuilding against increasing Chinese dominance
item.description: The heads of four major labor unions are calling on President Donald Trump to boost American shipbuilding and enforce tariffs and other “strong penalties” against China for its increasing dominance.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Pakistan's capital and a nearby city in an effort that the Afghan Embassy in Islamabad described on Wednesday as a push to force the expulsion of all Afghan refugees from the country.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico president says her government requested US surveillance drone flights
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday that surveillance drone flights by the U.S. government over Mexico are occurring in collaboration with and at the request of her government. They come as U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A look at the 8 Latin American crime groups designated as terrorist organizations by the US
item.description: The United States government is formally designating eight Latin American organized crime groups that also operate in the U_S_ to be “foreign terrorist organizations.”.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italian Prime Minister Meloni visits Pope Francis in hospital, says he's in good spirits
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in hospital on Wednesday and reported he was full of good humor following his diagnosis of pneumonia and a bronchial infection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hawley calls for new inspector general to investigate Ukraine spending
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) pledged to introduce legislation to track U.S. dollars designated for Ukraine in its war against Russia, after citing the possibility of corrupt spending under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro charged over alleged coup that included a plan to poison Lula
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazil's prosecutor-general on Tuesday formally charged former President Jair Bolsonaro with attempting a coup to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, in a plot that included a plan to poison his successor and current President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and kill a Sup
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A top Hamas official says the militant group will free six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and return the bodies of four others on Thursday, a surprise acceleration in releases apparently in trade for Israel’s allowing mobile homes and construction equipment into the devastated Gaza
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio makes progress with Russia after Trump promised to bring end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Secretary of State Macro Rubio said first step in ending the war in Ukraine is reestablishing diplomacy with Russia by reopening embassies in Moscow and Washington, as EU watches with concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Toronto plane crash: Harrowing video shows Delta plane erupting into fireball, flipping upside down
item.description: All 80 people aboard the Delta plane that flipped upside down and crash landed in Toronto survived, but three, including a child, are critically injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Russian, US officials meet in Saudi Arabia to begin talks on Ukraine war without officials from Kyiv
item.description: Senior Russian and American officials met in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to begin talks on improving relations and negotiating an end to Moscow's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: KHIAM, Lebanon (AP) — Sabah Abdullah comes to her hometown in Lebanon every morning and sits next to her destroyed home. She is waiting for experts from Hezbollah's construction arm to compensate her for the damage caused by the Israel-Hezbollah war that has left her homeless.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea slams US and Asian rivals for pursuing 'absurd' plans to denuclearize North
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday criticized the United States and Asian neighbors for pursuing the “absurd” goal of denuclearizing the North and said it will push to expand its nuclear forces under the direction of its authoritarian leader Kim Jong Un.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist loses both hands while snorkeling in Turks and Caicos after shark attacks during failed photo op
item.description: A woman from Canada lost both her hands when she was attacked by a shark after trying to take a picture with the creature while swimming in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta jet crashes at Canada's Toronto Pearson Airport; crews responding to plane flipped upside down
item.description: Officials confirmed to Fox News Digital that Delta plane crashed at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon. The CRJ-900 jet, operating as Delta 4819, departed from Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 18 killed in stampede at New Delhi railway station in India as Hindu pilgrims travel to festival
item.description: In New Delhi, India, at least 18 people were killed in a stampede at a railway station. Many victims were Hindu pilgrims traveling to the Maha Kumbh festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation
